title: hymenocallis littoralis var. longituba
intro: Hymenocallis littoralis var. longituba, commonly known as the Sea Island Spider Lily, is a type of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and bulbs can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although proper identification and preparation are essential due to potential toxicity.
